title: Uzbekistan’s Race to Build a Digital State
gist: Uzbekistan has rapidly built one of Central Asia's most advanced digital states, jumping 71 places in the UN E-Government Development Index since 2020 to rank 63rd globally and entering the World Bank's top GovTech maturity group, raising unaddressed questions about who governs the resulting digital power.

insights.claim: Uzbekistan ranked 63rd of 193 countries in 2024 with an E-Government Development Index score of 0.7999, entering the 'Very High EGDI' group for the first time.
insights.speaker: Niginakhon Saida
insights.so_what: Signals a maturing digital-government market and infrastructure opportunity in Central Asia's most populous state.
insights.locator: In 2024, it ranked 63rd out of 193 countries

insights.claim: Uzbekistan moved up 71 positions in the E-Government index since 2020, raising its score from 0.617 to 0.958 and becoming the highest-ranked state in Central Asia.
insights.speaker: Niginakhon Saida
insights.so_what: The pace of digitization outstrips regional peers, positioning Uzbekistan as the Central Asian govtech leader.
insights.locator: What is striking is that the country has moved up 71 positions

insights.claim: Uzbekistan ranked in group A of the World Bank's GovTech Maturity Index, one of only 80 of 197 economies (41 percent) to reach that advanced tier in 2025.
insights.speaker: Niginakhon Saida
insights.so_what: Independent World Bank validation supports the case for Uzbekistan as an emerging digital-governance market.
insights.locator: In the latest World Bank GovTech Maturity Index

insights.claim: Uzbek citizens participate in one of Central Asia's most ambitious state-building projects each time they use smartphone-based government services, often without considering who governs the growing digital power.
insights.speaker: Niginakhon Saida
insights.so_what: Concentration of digital state power raises governance and surveillance risks that could shape the country's political trajectory.
insights.locator: Every time Uzbek citizens file taxes
